method: nk_null_model.py (archive) — 1000 trials, uniform random citation null, seed=42. Supplementary inline citation graph analysis for hub decomposition and PA ratio.
expect: NK tracking checkpoint at N=1203: K_avg ~3.3, hub z >350, PA ratio 1.3-1.5x. Sinks <25%.

actual.verdict: GENUINELY NON-RANDOM structure detected (all 3 metrics highly significant)

comparison_s487.hub_z_method_note: S487 hub_z=335.8 uses Monte Carlo method (different null distribution). S504 hub_z=166.6 uses nk_null_model.py (uniform random citation, 1000 trials). Methods NOT directly comparable. Both confirm extreme non-random hub dominance.

predictions_vs_expect.hub_z_gt_350: INDETERMINATE — S487 hub_z=335.8 (MC method) vs S504=166.6 (null model method). Methods differ. Hub dominance is real but z-scores not comparable.
predictions_vs_expect.PA_ratio_1_3_to_1_5: FALSIFIED — predicted 1.3-1.5x, got 2.91x. PA ratio RE-ACCELERATED after S481 dip to 1.38x.

falsification_criteria_from_s481.criterion_1: K_avg deceleration resumes at N=1200 (compaction-adjusted rate <0.001/L) — FALSIFIED: rate = 0.001582/L, continued acceleration
falsification_criteria_from_s481.criterion_2: PA ratio drops below 1.0x (no preferential attachment) — FALSIFIED: PA = 2.91x, strong preferential attachment
falsification_criteria_from_s481.criterion_3: L-601 hub fraction exceeds 35% (monopoly collapse) — NOT YET: 33.6%, approaching but below threshold

new_findings.pa_ratio_rebound: PA ratio oscillates: 3.26 -> 2.75 -> 1.38 -> 2.76 -> 2.91. The S481 dip to 1.38x was an anomaly, not a trend. Hub growth consistently outpaces N growth by ~3x.
new_findings.sink_decline: Sinks dropped from 24.8% to 22.5% — new lessons are increasingly connected, reducing isolation. Below random expectation (18.8%) by +3.7pp, meaning the graph still has more orphans than random would produce.
new_findings.gini_stability: Gini essentially unchanged: 0.6491 -> 0.6479. Citation inequality has stabilized at a high level, confirming a mature power-law structure.
new_findings.hub_dominance_accelerating: L-601 grew from 344 to 404 citations (+60) in 68 new lessons. That is 0.88 new citations per lesson — 88% of new lessons cite L-601. Hub fraction 29.4% -> 33.6%, approaching the 35% monopoly threshold.
new_findings.dominance_ratio_widening: L-601/L-526 dominance ratio: 6.8x -> 7.2x. The gap between the mega-hub and second-place continues to widen.
new_findings.l601_citation_rate: 60 new L-601 citations in 68 new lessons = 88.2% citation rate for L-601 in new content. This is HIGHER than the S481 rate of 56.4%.

diff: K_avg CONFIRMED at 3.35 (predicted ~3.3). PA ratio FALSIFIED: predicted 1.3-1.5x, got 2.91x — hub growth re-accelerated. Sinks CONFIRMED declining at 22.5%. Gini stable at 0.648. L-601 hub fraction 33.6% — approaching 35% monopoly threshold (criterion 3 from S481). Hub z methods incomparable across sessions. Key finding: PA ratio oscillation suggests measurement artifact in S481 dip, not real deceleration. L-601 citation rate in new lessons is 88%, driving continued hub dominance.
next_falsification: FALSIFIED if at N=1300: (1) K_avg rate decelerates below 0.001/L, (2) L-601 hub fraction exceeds 35% (monopoly collapse per S481 criterion), (3) PA ratio drops below 2.0x for two consecutive checkpoints.
self_apply: This measurement adds citations to L-601, feeding the hub attractor being measured. The 88% citation rate in new lessons is partially caused by NK tracking experiments themselves citing L-601 in every artifact.
